lede: A look at October 9 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including David Cameron and John Lennon, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Malala Yousafzai
events.desc: Pakistani Taliban attempt to assassinate outspoken schoolgirl Malala Yousafzai.

events.title: The Phantom of the Opera (1986 musical)
events.desc: The Phantom of the Opera, eventually the second longest running musical in London, opens at Her Majesty's Theatre.

events.title: Pope John Paul II
events.desc: Pope John Paul II greets the Dalai Lama during a private audience in Vatican City.

events.title: Chicago
events.desc: In Chicago, the National Guard is called in as demonstrations continue over the trial of the "Chicago Eight".

events.title: Uganda
events.desc: Uganda becomes an independent Commonwealth realm.

events.title: Panama
events.desc: A coup in Panama declares Ricardo Adolfo de la Guardia Arango the new president.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Siege of Antwerp comes to an end.

events.title: SS Volturno (1906)
events.desc: The steamship SS Volturno catches fire in the mid-Atlantic.

events.title: Universal Postal Union
events.desc: The Universal Postal Union is created by the Treaty of Bern.

events.title: 1740 Batavia massacre
events.desc: Dutch colonists and Javanese natives begin a massacre of the ethnic Chinese population in Batavia, eventually killing at least 10,000.

births.name: David Cameron
births.line: English politician, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom

births.name: Heinz Fischer
births.line: Austrian academic and politician, 11th President of Austria

births.name: Ivo Andrić
births.line: Yugoslav novelist, poet, and short story writer, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Che Guevara, Argentinian-Cuban physician, politician and guerrilla leader
Cyril N. Hinshelwood, English chemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Pope Pius XII, Head of the Catholic Church from 1939 to 1958
Ernest Louis, Grand Duke of Hesse, Grand Duke of Hesse and by Rhine from 1892 to 1918

observances.text: Christian feast day: Innocencio of Mary Immaculate and Martyrs of Asturias
